task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium location to New York City and time to January 15, 2025, at 03:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'New York' and the date/time '2025-01-15 03:00' in the Stellarium bottom status bar, Location window (F6), or Date/Time window (F5).

task_completion.description: Enable Atmosphere and Constellation lines in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Atmosphere (cloud/sun icon) and Constellation Lines (connected stars icon) to be highlighted/toggled ON in the bottom toolbar, or visually confirm constellation lines are drawn in the sky.

task_completion.description: Center the view on the Orion Nebula (M42).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Orion Nebula' or 'M42' in the top-left selection information text, with the nebula centered in the main viewing area (often indicated by selection crosshairs).

task_completion.description: Set Light Pollution to level 8 and disable location-based light pollution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Sky and viewing options' window (F4) on the 'Sky' tab. The 'Light pollution' value should be set to 8, and the 'From locations' checkbox must be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Set Light Pollution to level 1 (pristine sky).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Sky and viewing options' window (F4) on the 'Sky' tab showing the 'Light pollution' value changed to 1, resulting in a visibly darker, star-filled sky.

task_completion.description: Persist the pristine sky state in the software configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Configuration window (F2) on the 'Main' tab with the 'Save settings' button being clicked while the sky is in the pristine (level 1) state.

task_completion.description: Write a presentation outline containing the words 'New York', 'Orion', and 'Bortle' to /home/ga/Desktop/dark_sky_presentation.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) open with the file path '/home/ga/Desktop/dark_sky_presentation.txt'. The content must be a presentation outline that explicitly includes the words 'New York', 'Orion', and 'Bortle'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to configure the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Stellarium application window and its internal menus (Location, Date/Time, Sky and viewing options) should be visible in the screenshots, not just terminal commands modifying the config.ini file.

integrity.description: Verify that the presentation outline is a genuine attempt at an outline.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file should contain structured outline elements (e.g., bullet points, headers, or numbered lists) rather than just a single sentence copy-pasting the prompt requirements.

integrity.description: Verify that the screenshots of the sky are captured from the Stellarium application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots being captured or saved should clearly show the Stellarium interface or render engine, confirming they were generated during the session and not downloaded from the internet.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target location: New York. Target coordinates: 0.7105 radians latitude, -1.2916 radians longitude. Presentation file must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/dark_sky_presentation.txt. Screenshots directory: /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium.
